title: Documentation: favicon caching and activity-dot dormancy
description: Update docs/API.md or the relevant inline documentation to note: (1) FaviconLoader derives icons from DuckDuckGo (icons.duckduckgo.com/ip3/<host>.ico) using feed_url — source hostnames are sent to that service; (2) has_new and has_priority are decoded defensively and default to false until the backend ships them; (3) activity dots are for source rows only (not article rows, article detail, or source-detail header).
acceptance_criteria: FaviconLoader source and caching strategy documented
Dormancy pattern for has_new/has_priority noted so future developers understand why fields default to false
Scope note (source list rows only) is recorded

handoff_summary.completed: All three acceptance criteria met: FaviconLoader caching strategy documented; has_new/has_priority dormancy pattern noted with activation path; scope restricted to source list rows stated explicitly.
handoff_summary.remaining: None — documentation is complete and consistent with the implemented code.
handoff_summary.risks: None. Documentation-only change to docs/API.md; no runtime code modified.
handoff_summary.conflict_risks: Low. docs/API.md is a standalone documentation file with no sibling beads modifying it concurrently.
handoff_summary.design_decisions: Placed the new subsections at the end of the 'iOS client: Sources tab' section, after getCategories, to keep all Sources-tab client documentation grouped together. Cross-referenced the activity-dot dormancy pattern to the existing Category freshness dormancy section to make the pattern recognizable to future developers.
handoff_summary.known_limitations: The spec file referenced in linked_docs (specs/planned/source-rows-favicons-and-activity-dots.md) does not exist in the worktree; it may not have been created yet or may have already been moved to done/. This does not affect the documentation completeness.

conflict_risks: Low. docs/API.md is a standalone documentation file with no sibling beads modifying it concurrently.

execution_history.summary: Added two new subsections to docs/API.md under 'iOS client: Sources tab': 'Source favicons — FaviconLoader' documenting DuckDuckGo icon derivation, three-level caching, failure swallowing, and concurrent deduplication; 'Source activity dots — dormant until backend ships fields' documenting has_new/has_priority dot rendering, source-list-rows-only scope, and the decodeIfPresent dormancy pattern with activation path.

guardrails.template_text:
# Documentation Guardrails

Primary responsibility: Update documentation and examples that explain the assigned behavior without changing runtime feature behavior.

Allowed actions:
- Edit docs, examples, and explanatory text tied to the assigned bead.
- Focus only on files listed in the bead's touched_files and changed_files fields. Do not read or modify unrelated documentation.
- Align documentation with existing code and validated behavior.
- Identify when implementation or tests must land first and block with a handoff.

Disallowed actions:
- Change runtime behavior, production code paths, or feature logic.
- Invent undocumented behavior that is not present in the codebase.
- Approve code quality or test completeness as a substitute for review.
- Do not run the test suite. Testing is the tester agent's responsibility.

## Memory

**Read memory at bead start.** Before writing any documentation, run three searches using `$TAKT_CMD` (injected by the orchestrator):

```bash
$TAKT_CMD memory search "<bead topic keywords>" --namespace global
$TAKT_CMD memory search "<bead topic keywords>" --namespace feature:<feature_root_id>
$TAKT_CMD memory search "<bead topic keywords>" --namespace specs
```

Treat results as ambient context — apply relevant entries to inform documentation; skip entries that don't apply.

Do **not** write to memory — documentation agents are read-only.

Efficiency constraints:
- Do not read the full codebase for context. Focus on the changed files and their immediate surroundings.
- Keep documentation updates concise and proportional to the change.

Expected outputs:
- Return JSON with `outcome` set to `completed` (documentation beads always complete; use `verdict` for pass/fail) and `summary` as a one-line description of what was documented.
- Return JSON with structured verdict fields for every run: `verdict`, `findings_count`, and `requires_followup`.
- Use `verdict=approved`, `findings_count=0`, and `requires_followup=false` when documentation is complete with no gaps.
- Use `verdict=needs_changes`, set `findings_count` to the number of unresolved documentation gaps, set `requires_followup=true`, and include `block_reason` when implementation must land first or documentation cannot be completed.
- Keep `completed`, `remaining`, and `risks` as concise narrative context only; they do not override the structured verdict fields.
- Accurate updated docs and clear next-agent recommendations when code changes are required first.
